department: Karnataka State Police - Bengaluru City Police
description: Complete knowledge base for Police department - used for RAG-based grievance resolution

policies.rule: FIR must be registered within 24 hours of complaint — no refusal allowed by law
Missing person complaint acted upon immediately within 1 hour
Women safety complaints given highest priority — response within 30 minutes
Cyber crime complaints acknowledged within 24 hours
Traffic accident complaints registered within 2 hours
Noise pollution complaints acted upon within 4 hours
Theft complaints registered within 24 hours and investigation started
Domestic violence complaints must be handled with immediate protection order if needed

contact_info.working_hours: 24x7 for emergency. Office hours 10AM-5PM for non-emergency.

common_grievances.resolution: Call 100 immediately or visit nearest police station. FIR must be registered within 24 hours. Get FIR copy for insurance claims. Case number provided via SMS.
Call 100 immediately. Missing complaint registered within 1 hour. Photo and details shared with all stations. Check Trinetra app for updates.
Call 100 or visit police station. File complaint with written statement. Protection order can be issued if threat is credible. Women can call 1091.
Call cyber crime helpline 1930 immediately. File complaint at cybercrime.gov.in. Do not share OTP or banking details. Block your card if compromised.
Call 100 or women helpline 1091. Immediate response guaranteed. Protection officer assigned. Shelter home provided if needed. Case registered under IPC 498A.
Call local police station or 100. Complaint acted upon within 4 hours. Fine imposed on defaulters. Repeat offenders booked under IPC.
Call 100 or traffic helpline 103. Ambulance dispatched immediately. FIR registered within 2 hours. Medico-legal case (MLC) opened if injuries involved.
Police cannot refuse to register FIR by law. Send written complaint to SP or DCP. File complaint on ksp.gov.in. You can also approach magistrate directly.

faq.question: Can police refuse to register my FIR?
faq.answer: No. By law police cannot refuse to register FIR. If refused, send written complaint to SP office or DCP. You can also file complaint on ksp.gov.in or approach magistrate directly.

faq.question: How do I report cyber crime or online fraud?
faq.answer: Call cyber crime helpline 1930 immediately. Also file complaint at cybercrime.gov.in. Provide transaction details, screenshots and account numbers. Act fast to freeze funds.

faq.question: Someone is missing. What to do immediately?
faq.answer: Call 100 immediately. Go to nearest police station with recent photo and ID. Missing complaint registered within 1 hour. Details shared with all stations across Karnataka.

faq.question: How do I get a character certificate from police?
faq.answer: Apply online at ksp.gov.in or visit nearest police station with ID proof and address proof. Character certificate issued within 7-15 working days after verification.
